---
title: "Policy Brief"
subtitle: "Education Mobility in Uzbekistan"
author: "Research Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: false
  docx: default
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Why This Matters

Educational mobility determines whether children from low-education households can move into higher-productivity careers. Strong mobility supports innovation and inclusive growth.

## What We Measure

- Mobility trends by cohort and region
- Household and local drivers of mobility
- Reform-period changes after 2017

## Key Results

Insert final estimated effects after pipeline completion.

## Recommended Policy Actions

1. Target low-mobility regions with resource and teacher support.
2. Strengthen pathways from upper secondary to tertiary for first-generation students.
3. Expand remedial and digital support for students in low-parent-education households.

## Evidence and Caveats

This brief summarizes causal and descriptive findings from the full technical appendix.
